data.title: Rio Tinto Ends Merger Talks As Glencore Refocuses On Standalone Outlook
data.description:
• Rio Tinto has formally ended preliminary merger discussions with Glencore.
  • The talks related to a possible combination of the two global mining groups.
  • The decision closes off one potential corporate action path for Glencore, ticker LSE:GLEN.

Glencore, LSE:GLEN, last closed at £4.86, with the share price up 18.9% year to date and 41.4% over the past year. Over longer periods, the stock shows an 11.7% return over three years and 104.9% over five years, while the past week brought a 2.1% decline and the past month a 1.5% gain. This backdrop provides a sense of how the market has priced Glencore ahead of and around the now cancelled merger discussions.

With Rio Tinto walking away from talks, attention is likely to move back to Glencore's existing operations, capital allocation choices and any alternative corporate actions the company might consider. For shareholders, the key question becomes how Glencore positions itself after this decision and whether management provides clarity on priorities that could influence sentiment around LSE:GLEN.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Glencore by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Glencore.

📰 Beyond the headline: 2 risks and 3 things going right for Glencore that every investor should see.

Rio Tinto walking away from an all share merger closes the door on a potential step change for Glencore’s scale and asset mix, and keeps the investment case focused on what the existing portfolio can deliver. A tie up with a major diversified miner like Rio Tinto, BHP or Anglo American can sometimes bring synergies in project development, infrastructure and marketing. With that option now off the table, Glencore’s competitive position versus these peers rests more squarely on execution in core commodities and the timing of capital returns. The cancellation also arrives just as Glencore is set to release Q4 2025 earnings, with slightly lower 2025 revenue estimates, higher 2026 estimates and improved earnings forecasts, so the market may now pay closer attention to guidance and commentary on capital allocation instead of deal optionality.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Glencore Narrative

  • The end of merger talks keeps the focus on Glencore’s own copper growth projects and efficiency initiatives mentioned in the narrative, which had already been central to the earnings story.
  • The cancelled all share deal removes a possible shortcut to additional Tier 1 assets and diversification, which could have supported the narrative around long term earnings power if integration had gone well.
  • The narrative emphasises portfolio optimisation and potential asset sales, while this event introduces a separate question of future M&A options that may not be fully reflected in current expectations.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Glencore to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, so a deal driven increase in debt or ongoing high capital needs could be a concern without stronger profitability.
  • ⚠️ The loss of a potential large merger may limit near term diversification benefits if commodity markets turn against Glencore’s current mix.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow at 41.41% per year, which, if delivered, could support a case for focusing on organic execution rather than large transactions.
  • 🎁 The shares are described as trading at good value versus both estimated fair value and peers, which may appeal to investors who prefer Glencore as a standalone miner.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how Glencore frames capital allocation without the Rio Tinto option, including any updates on buybacks, dividends or targeted asset deals. Commentary around copper growth projects, coal exposure and the marketing business in the upcoming Q4 2025 results will also be important, particularly given the improved earnings estimates for 2025 and 2026. Any shift in analyst views on Glencore’s standalone plan, compared with diversified peers like Rio Tinto, BHP and Anglo American, could shape how the market treats the end of these merger talks.

data.title: Has Deere (DE) Run Too Far After 29% Year To Date Surge?
data.description:
• Wondering whether Deere at around US$600 a share still offers fair value, or if the price has already run too far ahead of the fundamentals? This article is built to help you answer that question calmly and clearly.
  • The stock last closed at US$600.84, with returns of 1.3% over 7 days, 16.8% over 30 days, 28.7% year to date, 21.4% over 1 year, 46.7% over 3 years, and 90.1% over 5 years. This naturally raises questions about what is already priced in and how much risk is now on the table.
  • Recent news around Deere has mainly focused on investor interest in its role as a major capital goods name linked to agriculture and heavy equipment, as well as ongoing attention on how its long term demand cycles and capital spending plans could affect future value. Together, these themes have kept the stock in focus for investors who are weighing growth potential against price.

Approach 1: Deere Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to a single value today, so you can compare that estimate with the current share price.

For Deere, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow (FCF) is about $3.56b. Analyst and extrapolated estimates suggest FCF could reach around $12.42b by 2030, with a full set of annual projections between 2026 and 2035 that are discounted back to today using Simply Wall St’s assumptions.

Adding these discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $674.85 per share. Against a recent share price around $600, the DCF points to an implied discount of roughly 11.0%. This indicates the shares screen as modestly undervalued on this cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Deere Price vs Earnings (P/E)

For a profitable company like Deere, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of earnings. It is a quick sense check to sit alongside the more detailed DCF work described earlier.

What counts as a "normal" P/E usually reflects how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower expected growth or higher uncertainty often line up with a lower P/E.

Deere currently trades on a P/E of 32.40x. That compares with a Machinery industry average of about 30.07x and a peer average of 28.18x, so the stock is priced above these simple benchmarks. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Deere is 38.71x. This is a proprietary estimate of what P/E might be appropriate once you factor in earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk, which makes it more tailored than a basic peer or sector comparison.

Because the Fair Ratio of 38.71x is higher than the current 32.40x, this approach indicates that the shares appear undervalued on a P/E basis.

data.title: Clinical Hold Lift On MAGNITUDE-2 Resets Risk Reward For Intellia Investors
data.description:
• Regulators have lifted the clinical hold on Intellia Therapeutics' MAGNITUDE-2 study, allowing the gene editing trial to resume.
  • The decision follows updates to safety protocols after rare adverse events were evaluated.
  • The cleared pathway enables expanded enrollment for MAGNITUDE-2 and continued work on Intellia's in vivo gene editing program.

For you as an investor looking at Intellia Therapeutics (NasdaqGM:NTLA), this regulatory step comes as the stock trades at $12.22. The share price sits against a mixed return profile, with the stock up 32.7% year to date and 6.0% over the past year, but showing a 67.5% decline over three years and an 80.8% decline over five years.

The lifting of the MAGNITUDE-2 clinical hold removes an immediate regulatory overhang and gives Intellia room to keep building its clinical data set. As you follow the story from here, the focus is likely to be on trial execution, safety updates and how the in vivo program progresses within the broader gene editing space.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Intellia Therapeutics by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Intellia Therapeutics.

Is Intellia Therapeutics's balance sheet strong enough for future acquisitions? Dive into our detailed financial health analysis.

The lifting of the MAGNITUDE-2 clinical hold is important because it removes a near term constraint on Intellia Therapeutics' in vivo gene editing program while keeping regulatory scrutiny firmly on safety. The updated protocol expands the planned patient enrollment and tightens risk mitigation measures rather than stopping the drug entirely. For you, that means regulators are allowing Intellia to keep generating data, but with closer monitoring after rare serious liver events were identified in a small fraction of treated patients. There are no fines disclosed here, yet any future safety signal could still lead to new restrictions or delays, which matters for a company that is still in the clinical stage and relies on continued trial progress to support partnerships and funding options. Competitors like CRISPR Therapeutics, Vertex Pharmaceuticals and Editas Medicine are also pushing gene editing programs forward, so trial execution and clean safety updates will be key factors in how Intellia is viewed next.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Intellia Therapeutics Narrative

  • The restart of MAGNITUDE-2 supports the narrative that a return to clinic can help reduce the safety overhang on Intellia's transthyretin amyloidosis program and keep its gene editing platform moving toward later stage milestones.
  • The need for protocol changes and tighter liver monitoring underscores that regulatory and safety questions remain, which could challenge assumptions around smooth clinical progress and timing of key readouts.
  • The specific conditions of the hold removal and any longer term regulatory expectations for liver safety are not fully captured in the broader narrative and may influence how future trial designs or label discussions develop.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Intellia Therapeutics to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Volatile share price over the past 3 months compared to the US market may amplify how the stock reacts to any new safety or regulatory updates from the MAGNITUDE program.
  • ⚠️ Intellia is currently unprofitable and not forecast to become profitable over the next 3 years, so extended timelines or further regulatory interruptions could increase funding pressure.
  • 🎁 Revenue is forecast to grow 53.89% per year, so successful continuation of MAGNITUDE-2 could support that outlook by keeping a key late stage asset on track.
  • 🎁 The shares are described as trading at 95% below an estimate of fair value, so any steady regulatory progress without new serious safety events may help close part of that gap over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on how quickly Intellia resumes enrollment under the updated MAGNITUDE-2 protocol, the frequency and severity of any new liver related events, and the tone of future FDA interactions on both the polyneuropathy and cardiomyopathy trials. Updates on collaborations and funding, especially if tied to progress in this program, will also matter for assessing how comfortably the company can sustain its R&D plans against ongoing losses while competing with other gene editing players.

data.title: Caterpillar RPMGlobal Deal Deepens Mining Software And Services Focus
data.description:
• Caterpillar has completed its acquisition of mining software company RPMGlobal, expanding its mining technology and planning tools.
  • The deal adds advanced data, scheduling and site management capabilities to Caterpillar's existing mining equipment and digital offerings.
  • The move supports Caterpillar's push to offer more integrated, software driven solutions to mining customers.

Caterpillar, traded as NYSE:CAT, now has RPMGlobal's mining software portfolio alongside its existing hardware and digital products. The company’s shares recently closed at $764.76, with returns of 3.0% over the past week and 18.2% over the past 30 days. Over 1 year, NYSE:CAT is up 119.0%, and returns over 3 and 5 years are very large.

For investors following NYSE:CAT, the RPMGlobal acquisition adds more exposure to software and data driven services within the mining segment. As Caterpillar integrates these tools into its wider offering, key areas to monitor include customer adoption, contract wins that bundle hardware and software, and how consistently the company executes on technology centric projects with mining clients.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Caterpillar by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Caterpillar.

We've flagged 2 risks for Caterpillar. See which could impact your investment.

For Caterpillar, RPMGlobal brings planning, scheduling and site-management software that sits directly on top of its mining trucks, loaders and autonomous systems. That is important because value in mining is increasingly shifting toward how efficiently fleets are planned and operated, not just which brand of truck is on site. By owning the planning layer, Caterpillar can aim for deeper, longer-term relationships where customers buy bundled equipment, software and services on a single mine-wide platform. It also moves Caterpillar closer to competitors like Komatsu and Hitachi, which have been investing in automation and digital tools around their fleets. The acquisition fits with the company’s broader push into higher-margin, recurring software and services at a time when it already has a record US$51.2b backlog and growing exposure to data-center related power demand. For you as an investor, the key question is whether Caterpillar can integrate RPMGlobal cleanly and convert more of its installed base to these software-led offerings, without letting complexity or execution risk offset the potential benefits.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Caterpillar Narrative

  • The deal supports the narrative that long-term earnings depend on high-margin services, by adding mine-planning and data tools that can sit alongside aftermarket parts and support.
  • Tariff headwinds and cost pressures already weigh on margins, and integrating a software business adds another layer of execution risk that could challenge the margin improvement story if costs run ahead of revenue.
  • The growing role of autonomous vehicles and site-wide data platforms in mining is only partly reflected in the existing narrative, so RPMGlobal may introduce upside or complexity that is not fully captured there yet.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Caterpillar to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Integration risk, as bringing RPMGlobal’s software, culture and product roadmap into a large industrial group could lead to delays or cost overruns if not managed tightly.
  • ⚠️ Existing headwinds, including roughly US$2.6b of tariff-related costs in 2026, may limit how much margin benefit Caterpillar can actually realize from higher-value software over the near term.
  • 🎁 The combination of RPMGlobal’s planning tools with Caterpillar’s equipment and autonomous vehicles could support more sticky, long-duration customer contracts and a higher mix of recurring revenue.
  • 🎁 Strong demand trends in mining and data-center infrastructure, together with a US$51.2b backlog, give Caterpillar a broad customer base to which it can offer the expanded mining-technology suite.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how Caterpillar talks about RPMGlobal at events like the Barclays Industrial Select Conference, particularly any commentary on revenue synergies or bundled offerings. Watch for metrics such as software or services revenue tied to mining, adoption of autonomous and data-driven solutions across major customers, and contract announcements that link mine-planning software with equipment sales. It is also worth tracking how competitors such as Komatsu, Hitachi and Deere position their own digital mining platforms, since that will shape pricing power and adoption rates. Finally, keep an eye on whether tariff-related costs or broader cost inflation make it harder for Caterpillar to convert higher-value software capabilities into sustained margin improvement.

Approach 1: Danaher Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value. For Danaher, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections and an assumed cost of equity.

Danaher’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $5.1b. Analyst estimates and extrapolations suggest free cash flow could reach around $7.7b by 2029, with ten year projections supplied by a mix of analyst inputs for the earlier years and Simply Wall St extrapolations for the later years. All of these projected cash flows, quoted in $, are discounted back to reflect today’s value.

On this basis, the DCF model produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $229.39 per share. Compared with the current share price of roughly $206, the model points to around a 10.0% discount, which suggests the shares are modestly undervalued on this cash flow view.

Approach 1: LeMaitre Vascular Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes forecasts of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required return rate, aiming to estimate what the business might be worth in today’s dollars.

For LeMaitre Vascular, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $65.56 million. Analysts provide free cash flow estimates out to 2030, with Simply Wall St extrapolating beyond the earlier analyst inputs. By 2030, projected free cash flow is $79.20 million, with a series of annual figures between 2026 and 2035 that are discounted back to today.

Pulling all of those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $68.56 per share. Compared to the recent share price of around $89.25, the DCF output implies the stock is roughly 30.2% overvalued on this model.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: LeMaitre Vascular Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like LeMaitre Vascular, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about valuation because it links what you pay per share to the earnings the business is currently generating. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to support a lower, more conservative P/E as a "normal" range.

Savara (SVRA) is back on investor radars after resubmitting its biologics license application, completing a tech transfer to Fujifilm, and aligning key analytical protocols with the FDA.

Preferred price to book of 15x, is it justified?

On a P/B of 15x, Savara trades well above the broader US Biotechs average of 2.6x, so the market is clearly paying up for this name.

P/B compares the company’s market value to its book value. It is often used for asset light, pre revenue or unprofitable biopharma businesses where traditional earnings metrics are less useful. For Savara, this high multiple sits alongside meaningful losses of $115.645m and no reported revenue, so the valuation appears to be anchored more to expectations than current financials.
